definition: Represent each atomic environment or whole structure with a symmetry-invariant atomistic descriptor such as SOAP, then use the SOAP kernel or a precomputed SOAP-derived distance as train-eval similarity. This is appropriate when novelty is structural and local chemical environment overlap matters more than formula overlap alone.
example_usage: python metric_to_similarity.py --input soap_metric.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --metric-kind similarity --metric-col soap_kernel

evidence.summary: The paper introduces SOAP as a similarity method for atomic neighbourhood environments with symmetry invariance.
SOAP is defined as a rotationally invariant smooth similarity kernel between atomic environments.
The authors report that SOAP gives faithful and robust atomistic representations for fitting potential energy surfaces.

limitations: SOAP parameters, cutoffs, species handling, and structure aggregation choices affect the resulting axis.
The bundled example converts precomputed SOAP metrics; it does not compute SOAP descriptors itself.
Formula-level novelty and local-environment novelty can disagree and should often be reported separately.
quality_gates: Report SOAP implementation, cutoff radius, radial/angular basis sizes, species treatment, and structure aggregation method.
Verify kernel values are bounded and comparable across train and evaluation structures.
Compare against formula-composition similarity when both composition and geometry matter.
